title: hieracium schraderi subsp. glabratum
intro: Hieracium schraderi subsp. glabratum, also known as a species of hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and even exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Hieracium schraderi subsp. glabratum is often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
